--- SOURCE 1: Guidelines – Southeastern Archaeological Conference ---
URL: https://www.southeasternarchaeology.org/grants-awards/grants/requirements/

SUMMARY:
SEAC offers grants ($1,000-$5,000) for public outreach projects in the Southeast, open to anyone, with a 6-page application due January 13.

FULL CONTENT:
![Southeastern Archaeological Conference](https://www.southeasternarchaeology.org/wp-content/uploads/header-seac2.gif)

**The 2024-2025 Grant Cycle opens Friday, October 25th and closes Monday, January 13 at 11:59 pm.**

The Southeastern Archaeological Conference, in order to promote public awareness of archaeology in the Southeast, supports a program of small grants to finance public outreach projects. SEAC will provide annual awards in an amount not to exceed $5,000. Applicants can request from $1,000 to $5,000 depending on the scope and impact of the project. Based on the application pool and the competitive review process, the review committee may decide to award more than one application, not exceeding $5,000 in total dollars awarded. If less than $5,000 is awarded during a grant cycle, unused funds will be returned to SEAC and will not be available to make future awards.

SUMMARY:

The [American Battlefield Protection Program](https://www.nps.gov/orgs/2287/index.htm) (ABPP) promotes the preservation and interpretation of America's battlefields and sites of conflict. It supports community-driven stewardship of historic resources. Visit the ABPP website to learn about the four types of grants it administers:

[State, Tribal, and Local Plans & Grants Division](https://www.nps.gov/orgs/1623/index.htm) (STLPG) works with State, Territorial, Tribal, and local governments, educational institutions, and non-profits to preserve America's historic places and diverse history. Grants from the Historic Preservation Fund (HPF) support a wide variety of activities that enable recipients to protect and share significant cultural resources. # Scholarships and Grants

## Scholarships

Through its scholarship programs, SAA works to increase diversity in the field of archaeology, support the future of the field, and help today’s students become tomorrow’s archaeologists.

##### **Undergraduate and Graduate**

**[Native American Scholarships](/career-practice/Scholarships-and-Grants/native-american-scholarships-fund)*****Closed for applications. It will open for applications on October 1.***

The Native American Scholarships Fund (NASF) is an endowment established to foster a sense of shared purpose and positive interaction between archaeologists and Native Americans. Scholarships are open to all Native peoples from anywhere in the Americas, Alaska Natives, Native Hawaiians, and Indigenous Pacific Islanders. It supports the Arthur C. Parker Scholarship for Archaeological Training for Native Americans and the SAA Native American Undergraduate and Graduate Archaeology Scholarships. (Employees of tribal cultural preservation programs are eligible to apply for Arthur C. Parker.)

[**Historically Underrecognized Student Archaeology Scholarships (HUSAS)**](/career-practice/Scholarships-and-Grants/Historically-Underrecognized-Student-Archaeology-Scholarships)  
***Closed for applications. It will open for applications on October 1.***

The SAA Historically Underrecognized Student Archaeology Scholarships helps increase the number of under-represented minorities obtaining degrees in archaeology. It provides funding to minority archaeology students, helping them enhance their education and successfully prepare for a variety of careers in archaeology and heritage management.  

[**Cheryl L. Wase Memorial Scholarship for the Study of Archaeology**](/career-practice/Scholarships-and-Grants/cheryl-l.-wase-memorial-scholarship-for-the-study-of-archaeology)   
***Closed for applications. It will open for applications on October 1.***

Cheryl L. Wase Memorial Scholarship for the Study of Archaeology provides funding for an undergraduate or graduate education in archaeology. It is awarded to women who are residents of New Mexico and pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Anthropology with a specialization in archaeology from a fully accredited university in the state of New Mexico (Eastern New Mexico University, New Mexico State University, New Mexico Highlands University, or University of New Mexico).

##### **Graduate Only**

[**Matthew Tobin Cappetta Archaeological Scholarship Endowment**](/career-practice/Scholarships-and-Grants/matthew-tobin-cappetta-archaeological-scholarship-endowment)***Closed for applications. It will open for applications on October 1.***  
  
Patricia and Les Cappetta have established the Matthew Tobin Cappetta Archaeological Scholarship Endowment with a gift of $100,000 and it is their hope that others will be inspired to contribute to the endowment.  
  
Donations to this endowment support a scholarship that covers tuition, required fees, and books or course materials for one or more graduate students (Master's or Ph.D) to help them complete a graduate degree in archaeology or anthropology in memory of Matthew Tobin Cappetta.

**[The **Ofer Bar-Yosef Scholarship for Advanced Doctoral Students in Old World Prehistory**](/career-practice/Scholarships-and-Grants/ofer-bar-yosef-scholarship)**  
***Closed for applications. It will open for applications on October 1.***

The Bar-Yosef family created the Ofer Bar-Yosef Scholarship to honor and celebrate the life and remarkable archaeological career of Professor Ofer Bar-Yosef. Professor Bar-Yosef's research made foundational contributions to our understanding of human evolution and cultural development from the Lower Paleolithic to the development of Neolithic farming economies and sedentary villages, and geographically spanned the entire Old World from Western Europe to China.

The Ofer Bar-Yosef Scholarship acknowledges and supports excellence among advanced graduate students pursuing a doctoral degree on some facet of prehistoric archaeology in the Old World. The scholarship is intended for students who have already completed their coursework and all other requirements for the doctoral degree except the dissertation itself (research and/or writing). The Ofer Bar-Yosef Scholarship is given specifically to facilitate completion of the applicant's doctoral degree. The scholarship is open to students whose research focuses on Paleolithic, Mesolithic, or Neolithic archaeology (or their broadly equivalent temporal or cultural manifestations) in Europe, Asia, Africa, or Australia. The goal is to help promising advanced students to produce superlative dissertations in these fields.

## Grants

**[H. and T. King Grants for Archaeology of the Ancient Americas](https://saa.org/career-practice/Scholarships-and-Grants/H-and-T-King-Grants-for-Archaeology-of-the-Ancient-Americas)*****Not currently accepting proposals.***

The H. and T. King Grant for Archaeology of the Ancient Americas, administered by the Society for American Archaeology, supports archaeological projects across Latin America (exclusive of the US) that show promise for transformative contributions to the understanding of the indigenous cultures from Mesoamerica, Central and South America, and the Caribbean.

The AIA’s mission includes supporting and promoting archaeological research and its dissemination. To that end, the AIA offers a variety of scholarships, fellowships, and grants for excavation, research, publication, and site preservation. Scroll down for a list of our grants, application requirements, and deadlines.

Each year, the Institute also presents a number of awards to archaeologists, educators, authors, and others whose work has had a positive impact on the field of archaeology.

Want to find other opportunities? We have included a lists of grants and fellowships from non-AIA sources.[Click here to view.](https://www.archaeological.org/programs/professionals/grants-awards/other/)

### AIA Awards

* * *

[Anna Marguerite McCann Award for Fieldwork Reports](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/mccann-field-report-award/)
**Deadline:**March 15, 2025

The award committee will recommend a report on fieldwork (e.g. excavation and/or survey) that it deems most worthy of recognition each year.

[Award for Outstanding Work in Digital Archaeology](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/digital-archaeology-award/)
**Deadline:**September 15, 2025

[Conservation and Heritage Management Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/conservation-and-heritage-management-award/)
**Deadline:**September 15, 2025

[Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/excellence-in-undergraduate-teaching-award/)
**Deadline:**June 1, 2025

[Felicia A. Holton Book Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/holton-book-award/)
**Deadline:**February 1, 2025

[Gold Medal Award for Distinguished Archaeological Achievement](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/gold-medal-award/)
**Deadline:**November 15, 2025

[Graduate Student Paper Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/graduate-student-paper-award/)
**Deadline:**December 1, 2025

[James R. Wiseman Book Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/wiseman-book-award/)
**Deadline:**March 15, 2025

[Martha and Artemis Joukowsky Distinguished Service Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/joukowsky-distinguished-service-award/)
**Deadline:**September 15, 2025

Granted to those AIA members who, through their sustained and exceptional volunteer efforts, have furthered the work of the Institute and increased public awareness regarding its mission

[Outstanding Public Service Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/outstanding-public-service-award/)
**Deadline:**April 1, 2025

[Pomerance Award for Scientific Contributions to Archaeology](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/pomerance-award-for-scientific-contributions/) - Updated
**Deadline:**August 15, 2025

[Poster Awards](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/poster-awards/)

[Public Engagement Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/public-engagement-award/) - New
**Deadline:**October 1, 2025

[The AIA «Telestes» Award for Material Culture Research in Ancient Music and Dance](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/the-aia-telestes-award-for-material-culture-research-in-ancient-music-and-dance/)
**Deadline:**September 15, 2025

### Fellowships

* * *

[Anna C. & Oliver C. Colburn Fellowships](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/colburn-fellowships/) - Updated
**Deadline:**December 15, 2025

To support study at the American School of Classical Studies at Athens

[Harriet and Leon Pomerance Fellowship](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/pomerance-fellowship/) - Updated
**Deadline:**December 15, 2025

To support a project relating to Aegean Bronze Age archaeology.

[Helen M. Woodruff Fellowship of the AIA and the American Academy in Rome](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/woodruff-fellowship/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2023

To support a Rome Prize Fellowship for the study of archaeology or classical studies every other year.

[John R. Coleman Traveling Fellowship](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/coleman-traveling-fellowship/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2025

To honor the memory of John R. Coleman by supporting travel and study in Italy, the western Mediterranean, or North Africa.

[Olivia James Traveling Fellowship](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/james-traveling-fellowship/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2025

For travel and study in Greece, Cyprus, the Aegean Islands, Sicily, southern Italy, Asia Minor, and Mesopotamia.

[The Archaeology of Portugal Fellowship](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/archaeology-of-portugal-fellowship/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2024

To support projects relating to the archaeology of Portugal

### C. Brian Rose AIA/DAI Exchange Fellowships

* * *

[AIA Fellowship for Study in the U.S.](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/aia-dai-exchange-fellowship-us/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2025

[DAI Fellowship for Study in Berlin](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/aia-dai-exchange-fellowship-berlin/)
**Deadline:**November 30, 2024

The Archaeological Institute of America and the German Archaeological Institute (Deutsches Archeologisches Institut) offer reciprocal study fellowships. The program encourages and supports scholarship on various aspects of archaeology and promotes contact between North American and German archaeologists.

### Grants

* * *

[AIA-NEH Grants for Post-Fieldwork Research and Publication](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/aia-neh-post-fieldwork-grants/) - Program ended
Multiple small grants to support post-fieldwork activities including research and publication preparation

[Ellen and Charles Steinmetz Endowment for Archaeology](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/the-ellen-and-charles-steinmetz-endowment-fund-for-archaeology/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2025

The Steinmetz Fund will support innovative uses of technology in archaeological research.

[Julie Herzig Desnick Endowment for Archaeological Field Surveys](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/herzig-desnick-endowment/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2025

The Herzig Desnick Fund provides grants specifically dedicated to exploratory archaeological field surveys, especially for early stage work.

[Kathleen and David Boochever Endowment for Fieldwork and Scientific Analyses](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/boochever-endowment/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2025

The Boochever Fund will support field and laboratory research.

[Richard C. MacDonald Iliad Endowment for Archaeological Research](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/macdonald-iliad-endowment/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2025

This grant will be awarded to projects that support research in regions that supply context to the study of Troy and generally help elucidate the Trojan War and its impact on ancient Mediterranean civilization. This can include but is not limited to Anatolia (modern Turkey), southeastern Europe, the Aegean and Crete during the third to first millennia B.C.E.

[Site Preservation Grant](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/site-preservation/)
**Deadline:**November 1, 2025

The Site Preservation Program provides grants of up to $15,000 to support innovative projects that aid in the preservation of threatened archaeological sites.

### Publication Grants

* * *

[Samuel H. Kress Grants for Research and Publication in Classical Art and Architecture](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/kress-grants/)
These grants fund publication preparation, or research leading to publication, undertaken by professional members of the AIA.

[The AIA Publication Subvention Program](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/publication-subvention-program/) - Updated
**Deadline:**March 1, 2025

This program offers subventions from the AIA's von Bothmer Publication Fund in support of new book-length publications in the field of Greek, Roman, and Etruscan archaeology and art history.

### Scholarships & Travel Grants

* * *

[Anna Marguerite McCann Diversity Student Travel Grants](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/diversity-student-travel/) - New
**Deadline:**November 15, 2024

These grants are meant to assist undergraduate or graduate students who qualify under the category of “underrepresented minority” with their travel expenses to attend the AIA Annual Meeting.

[Archaeological Field School Scholarships](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/waldbaum-scholarship/)
**Deadline:**March 1, 2025

Scholarships to assist students with the expenses associated with participation in archaeological field schools including the Jane C. Waldbaum Field School Scholarships.

[Elizabeth Bartman Museum Internship](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/bartman-museum-internship/)
**Deadline:**April 1, 2026

A scholarship established in honor of AIA Honorary President Elizabeth Bartman to assist students or those who have recently completed a master's degree with the expenses associated with participating in a museum internship either in the United States or abroad.

[Graduate Student Travel Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/graduate-student-travel-award/)
**Deadline:**December 1, 2024

These grants are to assist graduate students presenting papers at the AIA Annual Meeting with their travel expenses.

### Society Awards

* * *

[Foot Soldier Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/foot-soldier-award/)
**Deadline:**December 1, 2025

This award recognizes dedicated members who provide years of service in multiple capacities at the Local Society level.

[Golden Trowel Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/golden-trowel-award/)
**Deadline:**July 1, 2025

The Golden Trowel Award honors the society whose membership proportionally increases the most during the past fiscal year (July 1st to June 30th).

[Lecture Flyer, Website, and Media Contests](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/lecture-flyer-and-website-contest/)
**Deadline:**December 1, 2025

Every year the AIA rewards the societies that produce the best flyer and website, as communicating information to the public about upcoming events is one of the main goals of each society.

[Life Saver Award](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/life-saver-award/)
**Deadline:**December 1, 2025

This award recognizes those individuals who persevere and show that even when things go wrong, it can all work out right!

### Society Grants

* * *

[AIA Society Outreach Grant Program](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/society-outreach/)
**Deadline:**April 30, 2025

The AIA Society Outreach Grant encourages AIA societies to undertake activities such as a teachers' workshop, a kids' archaeology fair, a symposium or event in a local library, or other project that promotes archaeology and the AIA's objectives for local community outreach.

[International Archaeology Day Grants](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/iad-society-grants/)
**Deadline:**September 15, 2025

Grants for AIA Local Societies for International Archaeology Day

support Us
----------

The AIA is North America's largest and oldest nonprofit organization dedicated to archaeology. The Institute advances awareness, education, fieldwork, preservation, publication, and research of archaeological sites and cultural heritage throughout the world. Your contribution makes a difference.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Identify various grant opportunities like SEAC and ASOR, which support public outreach projects, providing essential funding for community archaeology initiatives.
Collaborate with local nonprofits and educational institutions to enhance resource sharing, increasing project visibility and fostering community involvement in archaeological efforts.
Utilize platforms like GoFundMe to mobilize community support, allowing individuals to contribute financially to specific archaeological projects, enhancing public engagement and ownership.
